name: FLUX.2 [flex]
description: FLUX.2 [flex] is Black Forest Labs' fine-grained control variant of FLUX.2 — exposes tunable inference steps, guidance, and prompt upsampling for typography-heavy and production workflows.

examples.name: Typography & Design
examples.description: Typography-heavy advertising layout — flex is tuned for crisp text rendering

input.prompt: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra product advertisement, 'Ultra-strong titanium' headline, close-up of phone edge showing titanium frame, dark gradient background, clean minimalist tech aesthetic

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: High Detail Generation
examples.description: Crank steps and guidance for maximum detail when latency is not the priority

input.prompt: A detailed oil painting portrait of a Renaissance nobleman with intricate lace collar

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: Fast Draft
examples.description: Fast draft with prompt upsampling disabled — preserves the literal prompt

input.prompt: A simple line sketch of a mountain landscape

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

prompt.description: Text prompt for image generation or editing.

width.description: Width of the generated image in pixels (minimum 64). Omit to let BFL pick.

height.description: Height of the generated image in pixels (minimum 64). Omit to let BFL pick.

safety_tolerance.description: Tolerance for input/output moderation. 0 is the strictest, 5 the most permissive. Defaults to 2.

output_format.description: Output image format. Defaults to jpeg.

input_images.description: Up to 8 reference images for editing or multi-image composition. Each entry is an HTTPS URL or a data:image/...;base64,... URI.

prompt_upsampling.description: Whether BFL should expand short prompts before generation. Defaults to true on flex.

guidance.description: Classifier-free guidance scale (1.5–10). Higher values follow the prompt more strictly at the cost of realism.

steps.description: Number of denoising steps (1–50). Higher steps yield more detail at the cost of latency.
